description: Accelerate innovation, investment and productivity through world-class science, ensure that new and existing technologies are safely developed and deployed across the UK and drive forward a modern digital government for the benefit of its citizens. DSIT is a ministerial department, supported by 12 agencies and public bodies .

details.body:
Accelerate innovation, investment and productivity through world-class science, ensure that new and existing technologies are safely developed and deployed across the UK and drive forward a modern digital government for the benefit of its citizens.

DSIT is a ministerial department, supported by 12 agencies and public bodies.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Primary school children and teachers set to gain AI and digital skills as North East AI Growth Zone Taskforce meets to drive local opportunity
ordered_featured_documents.title: North East AI drive to boost local skills and business growth opportunities

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Isomorphic Labs – a company using frontier AI for drug design and development – is the latest to receive investment from Sovereign AI.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government’s Sovereign AI invests in British-founded AI company redefining how medicines are designed

ordered_featured_documents.summary: UK businesses are being urged to strengthen their cyber defences against fast-evolving AI-enabled threats.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government steps up action to strengthen cyber defences as UK cyber industry continues to grow

image.alt_text: Strengthening sovereign AI to stay secure.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: In a landmark speech, the Technology Secretary said there must be a “decisive move” towards backing more British tech companies as AI reshapes global power, security and prosperity.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Britain must secure greater control and leverage over AI to protect our national security in fractured world

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The UK government’s Sovereign AI is backing Ineffable Intelligence, co-investing with the British Business Bank to scale a UK-built, self-learning AI that can generate new knowledge and drive breakthroughs.
ordered_featured_documents.title: UK backs company building breakthrough AI that can discover new knowledge

image.alt_text: New £500 million fund will back AI founders, drive growth and create jobs.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Sovereign AI is the UK’s £500 million bet to back homegrown AI founders, drive growth and create jobs across the UK.
ordered_featured_documents.title: AI firms pioneering drug discovery, cheaper supercomputing and more get first backing through UK’s Sovereign AI

details.body:
The acting director general is a member of the Department for Education’s Leadership team and attends both the implementation and management committees.

The role is responsible for:

  • higher and further education strategy and policy
  • the adult education budget and 16 to 19 funding
  • review of Post-18 education and funding
  • further education provision, quality, restructuring and intervention
  • workforce development, upskilling and lifetime learning
  • analysis and data, including the exploitation of big data for further and higher education
  • sponsorship of the Office for Students and the Student Loans Company
  • careers and basic skills

details.body: The Director General is responsible for catalysing cross-government digital transformation from within the Government Digital Service. This includes digital strategy, performance and assurance, service transformation, the public sector adoption of AI, plus the foundations that enable transformation such as data, technical architecture, the service manual, and digital skills and capability in the civil service.

details.body: The Chief Scientific Adviser (CSA) is MOD’s principal adviser on science and technology, with responsibility for directing research through the MOD’s core science and technology research portfolio. The CSA reports to the Permanent Secretary, and is a member of the Joint Requirements Oversight Committee and the Investment Approvals Committee.

role_appointments.title: Professor Dame Angela McLean - MOD Chief Scientific Adviser

details.body:
The Government Chief Scientific Adviser (GCSA) is responsible for putting excellent science advice at the heart of decision making through:

  • The provision of proactive and demand-led science advice to the PM and Cabinet that is relevant, excellent, and delivered fit for purpose 
  • Working across government to implement science advice mechanisms that are efficient, effective, speak truth to power and are embedded irreversibly in government systems.

role_appointments.title: Professor Dame Angela McLean - Government Chief Scientific Adviser

details.body:
The National Technology Adviser (NTA) is responsible for: 

• providing technology advice to the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology and members of the Cabinet 

• working across government departments, including with the Government Chief Scientific Adviser (GCSA) and Chief Scientific Adviser (CSA) network, to support the science and technology landscape, accelerate innovation and champion policy delivery  

• building networks across industry and academia to draw the best and most influential minds into HMG planning and awareness 

• promoting the UK’s strategies and policies on science and technology areas externally and internationally

role_appointments.title: Dr Dave Smith - National Technology Adviser

details.body:
The Secretary of State has overall responsibility for the business of the department, including the Departmental Strategy, planning and performance, reporting and governance requirements. They have direct responsibility for departmental expenditure.

DWP is responsible for the administration of the State Pension and working age benefits system, providing support to:

  • people of working age
  • employers
  • pensioners
  • families and children
  • disabled people

details.body:
The Secretary of State for Scotland is the UK Government Cabinet Minister representing Scotland. They act as the custodian of the Scottish devolution settlement, represent Scottish interests within the UK Government, and advocate for the UK Government’s policies in Scotland. 

They also promote partnership between the UK Government and the Scottish Government, as well as relations between the UK and Scottish Parliaments.

Responsibilities include:

  • Industrial strategy delivery
  • Investment
  • Defence
  • Energy and North Sea Policy
  • Child Poverty
  • Oversight of Brand Scotland programme
  • FTAs and market access
  • Constitutional policy
  • Local Growth

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Ian Murray MP - Secretary of State for Scotland

details.body:
The UK Government Space Adviser is responsible for:

  • playing a key part in supporting the UK’s innovative and growing space sector
  • supporting the sector to engage with government on aspects of policy on space
  • enhancing the understanding of the opportunities and challenges facing the space sector

The role is supported by the Space Directorate in the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology, and can be reached at Space.Adviser@dsit.gov.uk.

Space has the potential to play a vital role in supporting the government’s ambitions to drive innovation to improve public services, create better paid jobs and grow the economy, and will be a key enabling sector in delivering the priorities for the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology.

The Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for the Future Digital Economy and Online Safety (shared with Department for Business and Trade) is responsible for:

  • Online safety and counter-disinformation

  • Tech policy and market facing technology, including skills

  • International strategy, including electronic trade
  • Economic security
  • Technology diffusion

  • National security and resilience
  • Geospatial Commission

The National Technology Adviser (NTA) is responsible for: 

• providing technology advice to the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology and members of the Cabinet 

• working across government departments, including with the Government Chief Scientific Adviser (GCSA) and Chief Scientific Adviser (CSA) network, to support the science and technology landscape, accelerate innovation and champion policy delivery  

• building networks across industry and academia to draw the best and most influential minds into HMG planning and awareness

The (temporary) Director General is responsible for the Digital Centre Design project, designing the scope, strategy and structure of the new digital centre of government. This is part of DSIT’s overall organisational transformation following the Machinery of Government (MoG) change of 24 July.

The CEO is responsible for the strategic direction, and operational excellence of GDS. They ensure the delivery of world-class digital services that transform the way citizens interact with the government.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive Officer, Government Digital Service

details.body:
The main responsibilities of non-executive board members are to:

  • attend meetings of the departmental board, chaired by the Secretary of State
  • provide over-arching strategic guidance and insight to the department

The officer provides leadership to the Government Digital and Data function across government and is responsible for leading the government’s digital transformation.

They also lead the Government Digital and Data function for the government by putting the right conditions and capabilities in place to enable departments to transform through digital and technology.

The officer is responsible for the strategic product direction and operational excellence of government products and services. They ensure the delivery of world-class digital services that transform the way citizens interact with the government.

The interim Government Chief Data Officer is responsible for fostering a sustainable, data-centric culture across government and the wider public sector.

They also lead the development and implementation of the government-wide data strategy, promote common data standards, and enhance data sharing across departments and the Government Data and Digital (GDaD) community.

The officer provides leadership to the Government Digital and Data function across government and is responsible for leading the government’s digital transformation.

They also lead the Government Digital and Data function for the government by putting the right conditions and capabilities in place to enable departments to transform through digital and technology.

The Interim Director General is responsible for the following areas:

  • international and economic security
  • security and online harms 
  • digital and data policy
  • cyber security and digital identity
  • digital infrastructure, including Building Digital UK

ordered_roles.title: Interim Director General, Digital, Technology and Infrastructure

details.body:
The Interim Director General is responsible for the following areas:

  • AI opportunities
  • AI security institute

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State  (Minister for Digital Government and Data)

details.body:
DSIT responsibilities include:

  • cyber
  • telecoms
  • space
  • economic security
  • Regulatory Innovation Office (RIO)
  • digital inclusion and skills
  • talent – entrepreneurial and investor

Her DBT portfolio includes:

  • business support for developing the digital economy  
  • DBT digital government 
  • One Login for business 
  • digital trade policy 
  • regulatory environment for digital enterprises 
  • labour market environment for digital entrepreneurs  
  • preparation for economic impact of transformative AI/AGI  
  • tech adoption and skills  
  • professional services 
  • COVID loan recovery
  • Smart data

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Digital Economy)

details.body:
DSIT responsibilities include:

  • domestic science and research ecosystem
  • international science and research
  • Horizon Europe 
  • research and development
  • life sciences 
  • quantum and engineering biology
  • tech and innovation across the missions
  • Talent - universities
  • Oxford – Cambridge Growth Corridor Champion

DESNZ responsibilities include:

  • nuclear
  • fusion
  • science and innovation for climate and energy
  • AI in the energy sector
  • research and development

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for Science, Innovation, Research and Nuclear)

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • AI Opportunities
  • AI Security Institute
  • Intellectual Property Office (IPO)
  • Semiconductors
  • Tech for Growth
  • Online Safety

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for AI and Online Safety)

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • public sector reform
  • digital products
  • data policy

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for Digital Government and Data)

details.body:
The Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State is a joint Minister between the Cabinet Office and the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT), responsible for supporting and providing assurance to the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister and Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster in the Cabinet Office and the Secretary of State for DSIT on the design and cross-government delivery of the digital ID programme. 

He will work closely with the Minister for Digital Government and Data, who retains ownership of related areas including data policy and other government digital products. 

The minister is responsible for

  • Digital ID

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Digital ID)

details.body: The Director General is responsible for catalysing cross-government digital transformation from within the Government Digital Service. This includes digital strategy, performance and assurance, service transformation, the public sector adoption of AI, plus the foundations that enable transformation such as data, technical architecture, the service manual, and digital skills and capability in the civil service.

ordered_roles.title: Interim Director General, Digital Transformation Group

details.body: The Director General for Digital Products Group is responsible for the strategic product direction and operational excellence of government products and services. They ensure the delivery of world-class digital services that transform the way citizens interact with the government.

ordered_roles.title: Interim Director General, Emerging Technology and AI

details.body:
The Interim Director General is responsible for the following areas:

  • cyber security and digital identity
  • Government Cyber Unit
  • digital inclusion and skills
  • digital infrastructure, including Building Digital UK

The Director General (DG) is responsible for the following areas:

  • analysis
  • commercial
  • communications
  • digital
  • estates and security
  • finance
  • human resources and transformation
  • legal

They are also the DG sponsor for the Matrix Programme.

The Women in Tech Envoy is responsible for leading the Women in Tech Taskforce alongside the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology’s Secretary of State.

The Envoy will:

  • agree the operating model and scope of the Taskforce, in consultation with the Chair and members
  • lead meetings of the Women in Tech Taskforce and to promote the work of the Taskforce through occasional public appearances, engagements, and participating in content and media campaigns
  • oversee the activity of the Taskforce and review its progress, to ensure that it is delivering the workplan agreed by the Taskforce and producing workable recommendations and interventions for government consideration - listen to and take account of the views of the wider Taskforce, and its rotating members to reach consensus on advice given to DSIT Ministers  - support members in delivering on activities agreed by the Taskforce

The role carries a time commitment of up to 20 days per year, at a remuneration rate of £1,000 per day, giving a maximum total of £20,000 per annum. This is consistent with comparable senior advisory roles across government.

The Women in Tech Envoy is responsible for leading the Women in Tech Taskforce alongside the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology’s Secretary of State.

The Envoy will:

  • agree the operating model and scope of the Taskforce, in consultation with the Chair and members
  • lead meetings of the Women in Tech Taskforce and to promote the work of the Taskforce through occasional public appearances, engagements, and participating in content and media campaigns
  • oversee the activity of the Taskforce and review its progress, to ensure that it is delivering the workplan agreed by the Taskforce and producing workable recommendations and interventions for government consideration - listen to and take account of the views of the wider Taskforce, and its rotating members to reach consensus on advice given to DSIT Ministers  - support members in delivering on activities agreed by the Taskforce

The role carries a time commitment of up to 20 days per year, at a remuneration rate of £1,000 per day, giving a maximum total of £20,000 per annum. This is consistent with comparable senior advisory roles across government.
